rationale: The previous attempt failed because the four ensures-conjuncts are the antecedent of an `==>` in the postcondition, not hypotheses in the proof body — so `assert(in_dom == r1.is_Some())` could not be proven. We restructure as `assert(BIG ==> det_equal) by { ... }` whose `by`-block introduces the conjuncts as local hypotheses, then case-split on `m.dom().contains(va)` to discharge the Some/None branches by unfolding `page_entry_to_map_entry` (via its `when_used_as_spec` open spec form) and `View::view` (in-module `closed spec fn`, so its body is visible).
